questions.text: How many lines of symmetry does a square have?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 lines of symmetry: 2 diagonals and 2 lines through midpoints of opposite sides.

questions.text: A circle has infinite lines of symmetry.
questions.explanation: A circle has infinite lines of symmetry because any line passing through its center divides it into two equal halves.

questions.correctAnswer: When a figure can be folded along a line so that both halves match exactly.
questions.explanation: Reflectional symmetry (line symmetry) occurs when one half of a figure is the mirror image of the other half.

questions.text: The letter 'A' has ________ line(s) of symmetry.
questions.explanation: The letter 'A' has one vertical line of symmetry that divides it into two mirror-image halves.
